scenario: Riverstone Bank operates an ad-hoc sub-process in Camunda 8.8 that scores 5000 transactions per day across several risk checks. Each inner flow writes a local variable named riskScore via its job worker. The team wants every completed inner flow to append its riskScore value into a list named results that is visible in the parent process scope once the ad-hoc sub-process completes.
question: Which configuration on the ad-hoc sub-process collects each inner flow's riskScore into a list named results that is propagated to the parent scope on completion?

options.text: Set inputCollection to riskScore and inputElement to results on the ad-hoc sub-process so each completed inner flow appends its score to results
Set outputCollection to riskScore and outputElement to results on the ad-hoc sub-process so each completed inner flow appends its score to results
Set outputCollection to results and outputElement to = riskScore on each inner service task so each completed inner flow appends its score to results
Set outputCollection to results and outputElement to = riskScore on the ad-hoc sub-process so each completed inner flow appends its score to results

a.text: Incorrect. The documented attributes on the ad-hoc sub-process for collecting inner-flow output are outputCollection and outputElement, not inputCollection and inputElement, which belong to a different element category.

b.text: Incorrect. The role of the two attributes is reversed: outputCollection defines the variable name that stores the collected list, and outputElement is the expression read from each inner flow's output.

c.text: Incorrect. outputCollection and outputElement are defined on the ad-hoc sub-process itself, not on individual inner tasks; the collection lives as a local variable of the sub-process.

d.text: Correct. outputCollection names the local list variable on the ad-hoc sub-process and outputElement is the FEEL expression evaluated against each inner flow's output, so completed flows append their riskScore to results, which is then propagated to the parent scope.

explanation: An ad-hoc sub-process collects inner-flow output by setting outputCollection to the target list variable name and outputElement to a FEEL expression that reads the desired inner-flow variable; the collection is created as a local variable of the sub-process and is propagated to the parent scope when the sub-process completes.
